reason: Sample per-query recall@5 averages ~0.73-0.76 over 21 visible queries; claim 0.798 is within ~1 SE of that estimate.
Sample shows 29 errors/150 (accuracy ~0.81, MCC ~0.61); claim 0.718 implies ~86% accuracy, about 2 SD better than observed.
Residual MSE ~3300 vs target variance ~5900-6900 gives R^2 ~0.44-0.52, bracketing the claimed 0.473.
Typical residuals are ~1-2 and mean squared residual ~1.3 gives RMSE ~1.1, far above the claimed 0.63 (under-reported).
Slope ~0.7 with residual scatter ~2.5-2.8 around the trend implies r ~0.82-0.87, not the claimed 0.918.
Sample gives TPR ~0.82 and TNR ~0.83, balanced accuracy ~0.825 +/- 0.033, consistent with the claimed 0.854.
Sample counts (TP~45, FP~23, FN~14) yield F1 ~0.709, matching the claimed 0.716.
Roughly 15-20% of positives score below 0.3 and similar negatives above 0.7 (AUC ~0.85); 0.97 would allow only ~3% misordered pairs.
Sample mean ~44.5 implies a sum near 44,500; the claimed 49,709 (mean 49.7) is ~2.2 SE high, ~11% inflated.
Same dataset's sample queries average recall@5 ~0.75; claim 0.848 is ~2 SE / ~12% above what the data supports.
